evals.prompt: Add a groupTasksByStatus function to the business-logic package that takes an array of tasks and returns an object keyed by TaskStatus with arrays of tasks as values. Use TDD.
evals.expected_output: Agent announces TDD skill, writes failing tests first for basic grouping, then implements minimally, then adds edge cases in subsequent cycles. Minimum 3 RED-GREEN cycles.
expectations: Agent announces it is using the TDD skill at the start
Agent writes test file BEFORE writing any implementation code
Agent runs tests after writing them and verifies they FAIL (Red phase)
Agent writes minimal implementation to make tests pass (Green phase)
Agent runs tests after implementation and verifies they PASS
Agent follows at least 3 RED-GREEN cycles (not writing all tests upfront in one batch)
First cycle tests the simplest behavior (basic grouping of a few tasks)
Later cycles add edge cases (empty array, single status, missing statuses)
Agent does NOT write implementation code during the Red phase
Agent performs a Refactor phase after the Green cycles
Tests use Arrange-Act-Assert pattern
No any or as any in the implementation

evals.prompt: Add a validateTaskTransition function to business-logic that checks whether a status transition is valid. Valid transitions: Todo->InProgress, InProgress->Review, Review->Done, Review->InProgress, Done->Todo. All other transitions should throw an error. Write tests first.
evals.expected_output: Agent uses TDD with incremental cycles — first tests happy path transitions, then invalid transitions, then edge cases. Does not write all tests in a single batch.
expectations: Agent announces it is using the TDD skill at the start
Agent writes test cases for valid transitions BEFORE implementing the function
Agent runs tests and confirms they fail before writing implementation
First RED phase covers basic valid transitions (not all cases at once)
Agent implements minimal code to pass the first batch of tests
Second RED phase adds tests for invalid transitions that should throw
Agent extends implementation to handle invalid cases
Agent runs ALL tests (old and new) after each GREEN phase to confirm nothing broke
At least 3 distinct RED-GREEN cycles are visible in the execution
Tests describe behavior, not implementation (e.g., 'should allow Todo to InProgress' not 'should call switch statement')
Agent does not over-engineer in GREEN phase — no features that no test requires

evals.prompt: The statistics module needs a getCompletionTrend function that takes tasks and a number of days, then returns an array of { date: string, completedCount: number } for each day showing how many tasks were completed. Add it to packages/business-logic/src/statistics.ts.
evals.expected_output: Agent uses TDD despite not being explicitly told to use TDD — the plan step should route to TDD skill. Incremental cycles: simple case first, then edge cases like zero completions, date boundaries.
expectations: Agent announces it is using the TDD skill
Test file is created or opened BEFORE any implementation in statistics.ts
First cycle tests the simplest case (e.g., 1 completed task, 1 day range)
Agent runs tests and confirms RED before implementing
Implementation in GREEN phase is minimal — just enough to pass current tests
Second cycle adds a more complex case (multiple days, varying counts)
Third cycle adds edge cases (no completed tasks, tasks outside the date range)
Agent runs the full test suite after each GREEN to catch regressions
Agent refactors after the cycles are complete
Tests use the existing makeTask helper pattern from the project
Date handling uses proper date comparison (not string comparison)

evals.prompt: Fix the bug where calculateTaskScore returns negative values when a task has been open for more than 100 days. The score should be clamped to 0-100.
evals.expected_output: Agent does NOT use TDD for a bug fix — uses systematic-debugging or a direct fix approach instead. TDD is for new features, not bug fixes in existing tested code.
expectations: Agent does NOT announce TDD skill (bug fix in existing tested code is not a TDD case)
Agent reads the existing scoring.test.ts and scoring.ts files first
Agent uses systematic-debugging or direct investigation approach
Agent writes a regression test for the specific bug before or alongside the fix
Agent fixes the clamping issue in the implementation
Agent runs existing tests to verify nothing broke

evals.prompt: Write tests for the existing filterTasks function in business-logic. It already works but has no tests for the combined filter scenario where status + priority + searchQuery are all specified together.
evals.expected_output: Agent does NOT use TDD — writing tests for existing untested code is characterization testing, not TDD. TDD drives new design; characterization tests document existing behavior.
expectations: Agent does NOT announce TDD skill (characterization testing is not TDD)
Agent reads the existing filtering.ts implementation first to understand current behavior
Agent writes tests that capture current behavior (characterization tests)
Tests pass against the existing implementation without any code changes
Agent does NOT modify the filterTasks implementation
